method_atom: Bayesian inference of the free parameters of a parameterized constitutive relation (a family of candidate models linking two conjugate state variables) from a small, heterogeneous set of noisy, indirect observations. A Markov Chain Monte Carlo sampler explores the posterior distribution while explicitly propagating systematic measurement uncertainties into credible ranges for the underlying parameters, and the inferred parameters are then tested for consistency against independent prior constraints from other measurement modalities.
object_atom: The dense-matter equation of state of neutron-star interiors — the pressure-density and derived mass-radius relation — constrained by observed masses and radii of six neutron stars (X-ray bursters and transient low-mass X-ray binaries), yielding nuclear parameters such as the incompressibility, the density dependence of the bulk symmetry energy, and the maximum neutron-star mass.
brief_reason: The Bayesian/MCMC estimation of a parameterized model's parameters from noisy indirect data with propagated systematics and external consistency checks is the transferable method; the neutron-star dense-matter equation of state and its nuclear parameters are the astrophysics-specific object.
